ordered_steps.description: Inspect files in module 'components' for naming collisions between source and target projects.

details.recommended_tool: Use aetherfusion scan to identify specific file-level conflicts, then manually diff the conflicting files.

ordered_steps.description: Identify files in 'components' that do NOT conflict with target files, and prepare a copy plan.

details.condition: Only files NOT present in target should be copied in this step.
details.status: BLOCKED — v0.2 is read-only; actual file copy is deferred to v0.3+.
details.note: List candidate files here with their source paths. Human review required before copying.

ordered_steps.description: Review all import/require statements within 'components' files to ensure they resolve correctly in the target project.

details.target_dependencies: Check if imported modules are available in the target project. If any are missing, add them to the target dependency list.
details.relative_imports: If the module uses relative imports (e.g., '../utils'), verify that the relative path is still valid after fusion.
details.status: Requires manual review — automatic import rewriting is out of scope for v0.2.

ordered_steps.description: Check if 'components' requires any config changes in the target project (e.g., new aliases, env vars, build config).

details.tsconfig_paths: If the source project uses tsconfig path aliases (e.g., '@components/*'), ensure the target tsconfig.json includes the same aliases.
details.env_vars: Check for any environment variables used by the module (e.g., API_URL, VITE_*) and document them for the target project.
details.build_config: If using Vite/Webpack configs, check for any module-specific settings that need to be merged.

ordered_steps.description: Generate a dry-run preview of all changes that would be made when fusing 'components' into the target project.

details.status: BLOCKED — v0.2 is planning-only; patch generation is deferred to v0.3+.
details.planned_actions: This step will eventually produce a list of create/copy/modify operations with before/after paths, enabling a human to review the full impact before execution.

required_human_decisions.question: How should same-named files in module 'components' be handled?
required_human_decisions.context: Source and target both contain a module named 'components'. Files with identical names exist in both projects and must be resolved.

options.label: Overwrite
options.description: Replace target files with source versions.

options.label: Namespace
options.description: Rename source files to avoid collisions (e.g., add '_fusion' suffix).

options.label: Skip
options.description: Skip conflicting files; only copy non-conflicting ones.

options.label: Manual Merge
options.description: Manually review and merge each conflicting file.

required_human_decisions.question: Should dependency conflicts allow updating package.json / requirements.txt?
required_human_decisions.context: 2 dependency version conflict(s) detected. Resolving these may require updating the target project's dependency files.

options.label: Allow Updates
options.description: Permit modification of package.json / requirements.txt to resolve conflicts.

options.label: Deny Updates
options.description: Do not modify dependency files; resolve conflicts manually.

options.label: Review Each
options.description: Review each dependency conflict individually before deciding.

required_human_decisions.question: Should routing conflicts be resolved by integrating into target routes?
required_human_decisions.context: If module 'components' defines its own routes or entry points, decide whether to merge them into the target project's routing system or keep them separate.

options.label: Integrate Routes
options.description: Merge source routes into the target project's routing system.

options.label: Isolate Routes
options.description: Keep source routes separate — may need a sub-path prefix.

options.label: Defer Decision
options.description: Leave routing as-is and revisit after initial fusion.

required_human_decisions.question: Should the original directory structure of the source module be preserved?
required_human_decisions.context: Module 'components' at source path(s): C:/Users/User/AppData/Roaming/Tencent/Marvis/User/oAN1i2WKBP6qZ8WmwJYcerUhktUU/workspace/conv_19e980c1b63_6ad95acd17eb/temp/demo-projects/project-b/src/components. Target path(s): C:/Users/User/AppData/Roaming/Tencent/Marvis/User/oAN1i2WKBP6qZ8WmwJYcerUhktUU/workspace/conv_19e980c1b63_6ad95acd17eb/temp/demo-projects/project-a/src/components.

options.label: Preserve Structure
options.description: Keep the source module's original directory layout when copying to target.

options.label: Flatten Structure
options.description: Flatten the module files into the target's existing directory structure.

options.label: Adapt to Target
options.description: Reorganize to match the target project's conventions.

blocked_actions: v0.2 does not modify any source or target project files
do not automatically overwrite target project files
do not automatically modify dependency configuration (package.json / requirements.txt)
do not execute build or test commands (npm build / pytest / etc.)
do not make any network requests
